list.title: App
list.description:
The root component of the Connect Four application. Responsibilities:

  • Orchestrates top-level routing using React Router.
  • Connects the UI to the global Zustand useGameStore.
  • Manages the transition logic between settings, active gameplay, and results history.
  • Global injection of GDPR-related components (CookieConsent, PrivacyFooter).
  • @component

list.title: App~OnlineGamePageWrapper
list.description: Safeguard wrapper for the Online Game Page. Ensures that game settings exist and mode is 'online' before rendering. Redirects back to settings if the state is invalid or missing.

list.title: App~SettingsPageWrapper
list.description: HOC-like wrapper for the Settings Page to handle optional URL room codes.

list.title: App~addGameToHistory
list.description: Memoized callback to record match results into the global history. Enriches raw result data with current session metadata (mode, bot difficulty, grid size).

list.title: App~handleSettingsSubmit
list.description: Processes the settings form submission. Determines the final game mode (Bot or Online) and navigates to the appropriate route.

list.title: App~handleStartClick
list.description: Resets the current session and directs the user to the initial setup.

list.title: App~navigate
list.description: Hook to programmatically trigger navigation between routes.

list.title: Bot
list.description: Creates an instance of the base Bot.

list.title: Bot#checkDiagonalDown
list.description: Checks for a diagonal win condition (top-left to bottom-right).

list.title: Bot#checkDiagonalUp
list.description: Checks for a diagonal win condition (bottom-left to top-right).

list.title: Bot#checkHorizontal
list.description: Checks for a horizontal win condition.

list.title: Bot#checkVertical
list.description: Checks for a vertical win condition.

list.title: Bot#checkWinner
list.description: Checks if there is a winner on the given board.

list.title: Bot#chooseMove
list.description: Asynchronously calculates the best move for the bot. First, it checks for an immediate winning move, then it attempts to block the opponent's winning move. If neither, it chooses the move with the highest heuristic score.

list.title: Bot#evaluatePosition
list.description: Evaluates the desirability of a specific cell position based on potential winning lines. Awards points for consecutive pieces of the given symbol.

list.title: Bot#getAvailableMoves
list.description: Returns a list of available columns where a move can be made.

list.title: CookieConsent
list.description:
A GDPR-compliant cookie and local storage consent banner. Manages user privacy preferences for game sessions and personalization. The component integrates with a global useConsentStore to persist choices and controls the availability of features like SignalR online play and nickname saving.

  • @component

list.title: CookieConsent~shouldShow
list.description: Determines visibility logic: show if the user hasn't decided yet or if they explicitly opened the settings modal.

list.title: EasyBot
list.description: Creates an instance of the easy bot.

list.title: EasyBot#checkDiagonalDown
list.description: Checks the board for four consecutive pieces in a diagonal line (top-left to bottom-right).

list.title: EasyBot#checkDiagonalUp
list.description: Checks the board for four consecutive pieces in a diagonal line (bottom-left to top-right).

list.title: EasyBot#checkHorizontal
list.description: Checks the board for four consecutive pieces in a horizontal row.

list.title: EasyBot#checkVertical
list.description: Checks the board for four consecutive pieces in a vertical column.

list.title: EasyBot#checkWinner
list.description: Evaluates the entire board to determine if a winning condition has been met.

list.title: EasyBot#chooseMove
list.description:
Asynchronously chooses a move based on simple probabilities.

  • 30% chance to attempt blocking the opponent's winning move.
  • 20% chance to attempt a winning move if available.
  • Otherwise, it picks a completely random available column.

list.title: EasyBot#findDefensiveMove
list.description: Scans available columns to find a move that would block the opponent from winning on their next turn.

list.title: EasyBot#findWinningMove
list.description: Scans available columns to find a move that results in an immediate win for the bot.

list.title: EasyBot#getAvailableMoves
list.description: Retrieves all columns that are not completely full.

list.title: GamePage~handleChangeSettings
list.description: Closes portal and triggers settings navigation.

list.title: GamePage~handleColumnClick
list.description: Processes a column click for the human player. Resets the move timer upon a valid move.

list.title: GamePage~handleEndGame
list.description: Closes portal and triggers results navigation.

list.title: GamePage~handlePlayAgain
list.description: Resets the game state and local timers for a rematch.

list.title: HardBot
list.description: Creates an instance of the hard bot.

list.title: HardBot#checkDiagonalDown
list.description: Checks for a diagonal win condition (top-left to bottom-right).

list.title: HardBot#checkDiagonalUp
list.description: Checks for a diagonal win condition (bottom-left to top-right).

list.title: HardBot#checkHorizontal
list.description: Checks for a horizontal win condition.

list.title: HardBot#checkVertical
list.description: Checks for a vertical win condition.

list.title: HardBot#checkWinner
list.description: Checks if there is a winner on the given board by evaluating all possible directions.

list.title: HardBot#chooseMove
list.description: Asynchronously calculates the best move using the Minimax algorithm. Wrapped in a setTimeout to prevent blocking the main UI thread during heavy computation.

list.title: HardBot#evaluateBoard
list.description: Statically evaluates the board state without looking ahead. Used when the Minimax algorithm reaches its maximum depth.

list.title: HardBot#evaluateCell
list.description: Evaluates a specific cell by checking all possible lines (horizontal, vertical, diagonal) passing through it. Assigns weights based on the number of consecutive pieces and unblocked paths.

list.title: HardBot#getAvailableMoves
list.description: Returns a list of available columns where a move can be made.

list.title: HardBot#isBoardFull
list.description: Checks if the board is completely full, resulting in a draw.

list.title: HardBot#maxDepth
list.description: The maximum depth of the Minimax search tree. Higher values increase difficulty but reduce performance.

list.title: HardBot#minimax
list.description: Recursive Minimax algorithm with Alpha-Beta pruning. Evaluates the game tree to minimize the possible loss for a worst-case scenario.

list.title: LevelBot
list.description: Bot difficulty level. Required only if 'mode' is set to 'bot'.

list.title: OnlineGamePage~handleRestartApproved
list.description: Handles the 'Play Again' request logic between two networked players.

list.title: OnlineGamePage~isGameFinishedRef
list.description: Ref to access completion state inside SignalR closures without stale data

list.title: OnlineGamePage~setGameFinished
list.description: Updates the game completion state both in state and in the reference.

list.title: OnlineGamePage~startGameWithData
list.description: Prepares parameters for the online match and switches view to the game board.

list.title: Player
list.description: Creates a new player entity.

list.title: Player#checkDiagonalDown
list.description: Scans the board for a diagonal win condition extending downwards (top-left to bottom-right).

list.title: Player#checkDiagonalUp
list.description: Scans the board for a diagonal win condition extending upwards (bottom-left to top-right).

list.title: Player#checkHorizontal
list.description: Scans the board for a horizontal win condition (4 consecutive pieces in a row).

list.title: Player#checkVertical
list.description: Scans the board for a vertical win condition (4 consecutive pieces in a column).

list.title: Player#checkWinner
list.description: Master check function that evaluates the board for any win condition. Calls horizontal, vertical, and both diagonal checks.

list.title: Player#makeMove
list.description: Attempts to drop a piece into the specified column. Creates a deep copy of the board to maintain immutability (useful for React state and Minimax simulation).

list.title: PrivacyFooter
list.description:
A persistent footer component that providing quick access to privacy-related features. Contains a trigger to reopen the GDPR consent modal and a link to the full Privacy Policy page. Positioned fixed at the bottom-left of the viewport to remain accessible across different game screens.

  • @component

list.title: PrivacyPolicy
list.description:
A static legal and information page component. Displays the application's Privacy Policy, GDPR compliance details, and usage instructions. This component outlines the granular consent model, data processing via SignalR, and user rights regarding the erasure of local storage data. *

  • @component

list.title: ResultsPage~history
list.description: Selects the game history array from the Zustand store. Each history object typically contains: { id, winner, time, botLevel, mode }.

list.title: acceptAll
list.description: Grants full consent to all data processing categories.

list.title: addGameToHistory
list.description:
Adds a new game outcome to the session history. Uses an immutable update pattern to ensure React re-renders correctly.

  • @param {Object} gameResult - The result data of the finished game.

list.title: botColor
list.description: Visual color for the AI bot's pieces. Required only if 'mode' is set to 'bot'.

list.title: clearSession
list.description: Clears all session-related data. Useful when returning to the main menu to start a fresh configuration.

list.title: closeModal
list.description: Closes the privacy settings modal

list.title: columns
list.description: Number of columns in the game grid. Range: 4 to 10.

list.title: createGameSessionSlice
list.description:
Zustand slice responsible for managing the active game session state. It tracks current game configurations and determines if certain modes are locked (e.g., when joining a specific online room via a direct link).

  • @function

list.title: createHistorySlice
list.description:
Zustand slice responsible for maintaining the match history log. Stores an array of completed game results and provides a method to append new outcomes to the beginning of the list (Last-In, First-Out).

  • @function

list.title: declineAll
list.description: Revokes consent for all optional data processing.

list.title: firstPlayer
list.description: Determines who makes the first move. Required only if 'mode' is set to 'bot'.

list.title: formatTime
list.description:
Formats a total duration in seconds into a standard "MM:SS" string. Uses padding to ensure both minutes and seconds are always represented by two digits, adding a leading zero if necessary.

  • @param {number} seconds - The total number of seconds to format.

list.title: gameSchema
list.description:
Validation schema for the Game Settings form. Utilizes yup to enforce data types, constraints, and complex conditional requirements. This schema ensures that board dimensions are within technical limits and that specific configurations (like bot difficulty or online nicknames) are provided only when the relevant game mode is selected.

  • @constant

list.title: gameSettings
list.description: Current match configuration.

list.title: history
list.description: The list of match results recorded during the active session.

list.title: isModalOpen
list.description: Visibility state of the consent configuration modal

list.title: lockedMode
list.description: Forced game mode identifier.

list.title: mode
list.description: Defines the primary game mode. Must be either 'bot' (Local) or 'online' (SignalR).

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
Renders the interactive visual game board for Connect Four. Maps through the 2D board array to display empty slots, player pieces, and bot pieces with corresponding colors, animations, and hover effects.

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
A modal portal component displayed when a game session ends. Renders over the entire application using ReactDOM.createPortal to avoid z-index issues. Displays the result of the game (winner/draw), the elapsed time, bot difficulty, and provides actions for the next steps.

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
Renders the top status bar during active gameplay. Displays the current turn indicator (Player vs. Bot) and the elapsed game time, utilizing dynamic CSS classes for pulse and bounce animations to reflect the game state.

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
Renders a dynamic visual countdown timer for a player's turn. The component calculates the percentage of time remaining and adjusts its color palette and CSS animations accordingly (Green for >50%, Yellow for >25%, Red for <=25%).

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
Renders the multiplayer game interface and manages real-time synchronization. Uses SignalR (via the connection prop) to send and receive moves, handle opponent disconnections, and manage game restarts within a specific matchmaking room.

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
Renders the matchmaking lobby interface where users wait for an opponent to join. Displays the unique room code with a copy-to-clipboard feature and dynamically lists the connected players. Transitions automatically when the room is full.

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
Renders a summary card displaying the results of a completed game session. Shows the outcome (win/loss/draw), the game mode or bot difficulty, and the total elapsed time. Designed with a glassmorphism effect and dark mode support.

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
Renders the configuration form for starting a new game or updating settings. Integrates with react-hook-form and yup for schema-based validation. Dynamically adjusts available fields (like Online Mode or Nicknames) based on the user's GDPR cookie consent preferences stored in Zustand.

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
A reusable, styled button component used throughout the application. Applies consistent gradient backgrounds, hover states, and click animations (scaling) using Tailwind CSS to maintain uniform UI design.

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
The primary container component for the local game mode (Player vs. Bot). It orchestrates the game flow by managing the session timer, turn-based move timers, and bot execution timing. It also handles navigation callbacks for settings and results. *

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
Page component that manages the lifecycle of an online multiplayer session. Handles SignalR connection, room management (creation/joining), player synchronization, and game state transitions (waiting vs. playing). *

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
A page component that displays the history of game results for the current session. It retrieves the match history from the global useGameStore and maps through it to render individual ResultInfo cards. Provides a fallback UI if no games have been played. *

  • @component

list.title: module.exports
list.description:
The initial entry point component for the application. Renders the welcome screen with the game title and a primary call-to-action button. This page serves as the first step in the user flow before navigating to settings or game modes.

  • @component

list.title: moveTimeLimit
list.description: Optional time limit for a single turn in seconds. Range: 5 to 120 seconds. Empty strings are transformed to null.

list.title: name
list.description: Configuration for the persistence middleware. The store data is saved under the key 'ConnectFourStore' in Local Storage.

list.title: name
list.description: Configuration for persistent storage in 'AppConsentStore' local storage key

list.title: nickname
list.description: User nickname for online identity. Required only if 'mode' is 'online' and the GDPR consent for nickname usage ($canUseNickname) is granted.

list.title: openModal
list.description: Opens the privacy settings modal

list.title: playerColor
list.description: Visual color for the human player's pieces. Required only if 'mode' is set to 'bot'.

list.title: preferences
list.description: Granular user preferences for different data categories.

list.title: resetConsent
list.description: Resets the consent state to 'undecided', effectively re-triggering the privacy banner.

list.title: roomCode
list.description: Code to join a specific multiplayer room. Optional during creation, required contextually.

list.title: rows
list.description: Number of rows in the game grid. Range: 4 to 10.

list.title: setGameSession
list.description: Initializes or updates the current game session.

list.title: setPreferences
list.description: Updates granular preferences and sets the status to 'customized'.

list.title: status
list.description: Current consent status. Values: 'undecided' (show banner), 'customized', 'accepted_all', 'declined_all'.

list.title: useConsentStore
list.description:
Zustand store dedicated to managing user privacy and GDPR consent settings. It tracks whether a user has accepted, declined, or customized their data storage preferences (Cookies/Local Storage). This store acts as a gatekeeper for features like online matchmaking (requires session storage) and nickname persistence.

  • [Image of a state machine diagram for user consent status]
  • @module useConsentStore

list.title: useGame
list.description:
The core game logic hook that manages the Connect Four state engine. This hook handles the game board state, turn switching, win/draw detection, and orchestrates both human and automated (bot) moves. *

  • @hook

list.title: useGameStore
list.description:
The primary global state store for the Connect Four application. Utilizes a slice-based architecture to separate concerns between game sessions and match history.

  • Features:
  • Redux DevTools: Integrated for real-time state debugging.
  • Persistence: Automatically synchronizes the state with the browser's localStorage to preserve match history and settings across page refreshes.
  • @module useGameStore

list.title: useGameTimer
list.description:
A custom hook that manages the game session's total elapsed time. It sets up a 1-second interval that updates the game duration as long as the match is active. Automatically handles cleanup by clearing the interval when the game is paused, finished, or the component is unmounted. *

  • @hook

list.title: useGame~bot
list.description: Memoized bot/opponent instance. Acts as a factory creating the appropriate Bot class based on difficulty settings.

list.title: useGame~botMove
list.description: Logic for the AI or Online Opponent move.

list.title: useGame~checkWinner
list.description: Checks for a winner using the Player class logic.

list.title: useGame~createInitialBoard
list.description: Generates a clean board based on the row and column settings.

list.title: useGame~forceTimeout
list.description: Forcibly declares a bot victory if a timeout occurs.

list.title: useGame~player
list.description: Memoized human player instance.

list.title: useGame~playerMove
list.description: Logic for a human player dropping a piece. Checks for validity, updates the board, and evaluates win/draw conditions.

list.title: useGame~resetGame
list.description: Resets the game to initial state using current settings.

list.title: useSignalR
list.description:
Custom hook to manage the SignalR lifecycle for real-time multiplayer functionality. It initializes the hub connection, handles automatic reconnections, and provides a method to manually start the connection when the user enters the multiplayer mode.

  • @hook

list.title: useSignalR~startConnection
list.description:
Starts the SignalR connection if it is currently in a disconnected state. Updates the local connectionId state upon successful connection.

  • @async
